figures.caption: Fig. 1: OmniGen can generate attribute details while failing to recognize them (top), whereas BAGEL understands relational viewpoints that it fails to render (bottom).
Fig. 3: Scene-graph extraction pipeline, producing the final reference scene graph used for evaluation.
Fig. 10: Per-dimension tornado plots for JanusPro and MMaDA.
Fig. 2: Overview of our evaluation framework. Cross-Task Consistency is evaluated by aggregating Understanding and Generation performance anchored on the same scene.
Fig. 9: Per-dimension tornado plots for Gemini and GPT.
Fig. 11: Per-dimension tornado plots for OmniGen2 and Tar
Table 3: Understanding performance (U) measured via scene graph-derived VQA. We report the Overall Understanding score, followed by task-specific scores and accuracy restricted to matched nodes.
Table 2: Generation performance (G) evaluated on scene graph nodes. We report the performance for all nodes and matched nodes separately.
Fig. 12: Per-dimension tornado plots for Show-o and Show-o2.
Fig. 8: Per-dimension tornado plots for BAGEL and BLIP3o. Each plot shows attribute and relation category scores for generation and understanding, along with the corresponding imbalance.
Table 11: Mapping of super categories to COCO classes / corresponding attribute keys.
Table 10: Detailed predicate performance metrics. We report accuracy (Acc), balanced accuracy (Bal_Acc), F1-score, and Mean Recall (mR@K) at various thresholds.
